text.title: mobile-responsive Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: Layout horizontally scrolls on 320px devices; design breaks at edge cases.
antipattern.root-cause: Translating Figma px directly to CSS px instead of using fluid units.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `%`, `clamp()`, or `min(100%, 360px)`. Borders remain at 1px.

antipattern.symptom: Mobile styles override desktop ones via cascade weight; subtle bugs at edge sizes.
antipattern.root-cause: Starting from desktop mockups and bolting on mobile overrides.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite as mobile-first: base styles for <= 360px, then `@media (min-width: 768px)` for tablet up.

antipattern.symptom: Mobile users see no menu; tooltips never appear on touch devices.
antipattern.root-cause: Translating desktop demos verbatim; touch fallback omitted.
antipattern.fix: Provide tap/long-press equivalents; use `:hover, :focus, :focus-visible` triplet; consider always-visible variant on small viewports.

antipattern.symptom: Bottom-pinned UI hides behind the URL bar on iOS.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `100dvh` (dynamic) or `100svh` (small) / `100lvh` (large) depending on intent.
